2605.12263.title: Reconnecting Fragmented Citation Networks with Semantic Augmentation
2605.12263.paper_summary: Citation graphs are fundamental tools for modeling scientific structure, but are often fragmented due to missing citations of scientifically connected articles. To address this issue, we propose a computationally efficient hybrid framework integrating citation topology with large language model (LLM)-based text similarity. Using 662,369 Web of Science publications in Mathematics and Operations Research & Management Science, we augment the original graph by adding semantic edges from small, disconnected components and weighting existing citations according to textual similarity. Semantic augmentation substantially reduces fragmentation while preserving disciplinary homogeneity. Compared to embedding-only clustering, cluster detection on augmented graphs using the Leiden algorithm retains structural interpretability while offering multi-scale organization. The method scales efficiently to large datasets and offers a practical strategy for strengthening citation-based indicators without collapsing disciplinary boundaries.
2605.12263.paper_summary_zh: 引用圖是建模科學結構的基本工具，但由於缺少科學上相關文章的引用，這些圖往往會變得支離破碎。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了一個計算效率高的混合框架，將引用拓撲與基於大型語言模型（LLM）的文本相似性結合起來。利用662,369篇來自科學網絡的數學與運籌學及管理科學的出版物，我們通過從小的、不連通的組件中添加語義邊並根據文本相似性加權現有引用來增強原始圖。語義增強顯著減少了碎片化，同時保持了學科的同質性。與僅使用嵌入的聚類相比，使用Leiden算法在增強圖上進行的聚類檢測保留了結構的可解釋性，同時提供了多尺度的組織。該方法能有效擴展到大型數據集，並提供了一種實用的策略，以在不破壞學科邊界的情況下加強基於引用的指標。
2605.12263.author: Vu Thi Huong et.al.
2605.12263.authors: Vu Thi Huong, Annika Buchholz, Imene Khebouri, Thorsten Koch, Tim Kunt, Wolfgang Peters-Kottig, Tomasz Stompor, Janina Zittel
